title: Idho Idho En Pallavi Lyrics Sigaram
stanzas: <div>Idho idho En Pallavi Song Lyrics Sigaram

Idho idho en pallavi
eppodhu geetham aagumo
ivan undhan saranam endraal
appodhu vedham aagumo.

en vaanam engum pournami
idhu enna maayamo.
en kadhala un kadhala
naan kaanum kolamo.

en vazhkai ennum koappaiyil
idhu enna vaanamo.
parugaamale rushiyerudhe
idhu enna jaalamo.

pasi enbathe rusi allavaa
adhu indru theerumo..

Idho idho en pallavi
eppodhu geetham aagumo
ival undhan saranam endraal
appodhu vedham aagumo.

Andha vaanam
theerndhu pogalaam
nam vazhkai theerumaa.
paruvangal niramaralaam
nam paasam maarumaa.

oru paadal paada vandhaval
un paadal aaginen
vidhi maaralaam
un paadalil shruthi
maara koodumaa

Idho idho en pallavi
eppodhu geetham aagumo
ival undhan saranam endraal
appodhu vedham aagumo.

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song expresses themes of love and longing, reflecting on the beauty and complexity of romantic relationships.

In which movie does this song appear?

This song is featured in the movie Sigaram.

Who are the singers of this song?

The song is sung by the legendary duo K.S. Chithra and S.P. Balasubrahmanyam.

When was the song released?

The song was released on January 11, 1991.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Idho Idho En Pallavi, the lyrics weave a tale of love that transcends time, evoking feelings of nostalgia and romance. The song captures the essence of longing, as the singers express their emotions through heartfelt melodies. It serves as a beautiful backdrop to the unfolding love story in Sigaram, making it a memorable piece in Tamil cinema. The soothing composition and poignant lyrics resonate deeply with listeners, making it a cherished classic.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Idho Idho En Pallavi from Sigaram is a timeless melody that beautifully captures the essence of love and longing. With the enchanting voices of K.S. Chithra and S.P. Balasubrahmanyam, this song resonates with anyone who has ever experienced the magic of romance. Its soothing lyrics and captivating tune make it a must-listen for fans of classic Tamil music.
stanzaMeanings: Is my song ever going to become a melody? If he is your refuge, will it then become a scripture?
My sky is always full moon, what kind of magic is this? Will I see your love and my love together?
In my life, what kind of sky is this? Without any delay, what kind of trap is this?
Is hunger not a joy? Will it be resolved today?
Is my song ever going to become a melody? If she is your refuge, will it then become a scripture?
Will that sky come to an end, will our life end? Will the seasons remain unchanged, will our love change?
If a song comes to sing, will it become your song? Will fate change, will the notes in your song change?
Will my prayer go without your praise?
Is my song ever going to become a melody? If she is your refuge, will it then become a scripture?
